prompt:
Change request for the meter-feed importer. Background: our co-op's AMR head-end drops a fixed-width file every night (the MR-7 layout) and the import job reads it with the FixedWidthReader in this directory — offsets in, trimmed strings out. That part works and other jobs use it too, so its behavior is frozen. The problem is everything downstream of it: the new billing vendor wants TYPED values, and today ONE garbled line kills the whole import and a tech drives out to re-pull the file. I want the layout in config, converters per column, and every bad cell in the file reported in one pass — good rows still import.

Build the following as sibling .java files (no packages, no build tool, plain JDK — the dir runs with the source launcher):

RecordLayout.java — public final class with static RecordLayout parse(String spec). The spec is one line of comma-separated column entries; each entry is name:start:len:type (whitespace around entries and around the pieces is ignored; empty entries between commas are skipped). start/len are the same absolute-offset spans FixedWidthReader uses. Types: text (trimmed string), int (optional sign + digits, leading zeros fine, value is a Long), dec(K) (digit run with optional sign and K IMPLIED decimal places, mainframe style — '0012050' as dec(2) is 120.50 — value is a BigDecimal with scale exactly K), date(PATTERN) (parsed with DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern(PATTERN, Locale.ROOT), value is a LocalDate), code(A=ACTUAL|E=ESTIMATED|...) (single-bar-separated RAW=LABEL pairs; value is the label String). Type args never contain ':' or ',' — the splitter is allowed to be simple. Bad specs throw IllegalArgumentException with EXACTLY these messages: wrong piece count -> "bad column spec '<entry>' (want name:start:len:type)"; unknown type -> "unknown type '<type>' in column '<name>'"; duplicate name -> "duplicate column '<name>'"; non-integer / negative start / non-positive len -> "bad span in column '<name>'"; a spec with no entries at all -> "empty layout". Accessors: names() returns the column names in spec order, width() returns the rightmost end (max start+len).

RowError.java — a public record RowError(int line, String column, String message). line is the 1-based PHYSICAL line number in the file (blank lines count, they're just not records).

ReadResult.java — rows() returns List<Map<String,Object>> (one LinkedHashMap-ordered map per fully-clean row, column order, typed values), errors() returns List<RowError>, clean() is errors().isEmpty().

LayoutReader.java — public final class, constructor LayoutReader(RecordLayout), method ReadResult read(String content). Same framing rules as the shipped reader: split on '\n', tolerate a trailing '\r', skip blank lines, cells are substring(start, start+len) trimmed. But NEVER throw on bad data — collect. For each record line, check every column: a column whose span sticks out past the end of the line records "past end of line (<lineLength> chars)"; a cell that fails its converter records the converter message: int -> "not an integer: '<cell>'", dec -> "not a number: '<cell>'" (dec cells are sign+digits only, no dots), date -> "bad date: '<cell>'", code -> "unknown code: '<cell>'". Errors for one row appear in column-spec order; rows are processed top to bottom so errors() is globally ordered. A row with ANY error contributes no map to rows(); clean rows keep their relative order. The whole file is always processed — that's the point.

Do not change FixedWidthReader (RegressionTests.java pins it and must keep passing — you can run just that suite with `java RegressionTests.java`). TestMain.java is the full acceptance contract: it runs the regression suite plus the new-feature tests. Both test files are off limits. Done = `java TestMain.java` prints all PASS and exits 0.
